mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to risky assets and lifts equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days after rate cuts are observable.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative following FEDFUNDS declines, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which depresses risk appetite and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal has negative hit rate after a one-quarter lag.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both improve together, retail sales growth is being supported by employment gains, indicating stronger nominal demand that should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-PAYEMS improvement does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day returns, or if the combined signal's Rank IC remains below 0.03 for 2 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in INDPRO indicates industrial output contraction and margin pressure across cyclicals, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if manufacturing contractions are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered instances, or if the short-horizon hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST signals housing demand recovery, which improves construction activity, household wealth effects, and cyclical sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if rising housing starts fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe contribution is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains near the effective lower bound, inflation pressure forces a future policy repricing that hurts equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation > FEDFUNDS by 2 pp
regime_hint: inflation scare with suppressed policy rate
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate episodes are not followed by negative 20-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term loses significance in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS print after prior gains signals job losses before income data fully adjusts, which should weaken expected consumption and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's hit rate is not above random chance.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous strengthening of GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad real and nominal demand expansion, which supports earnings expectations and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail-sales strength does not lead to positive 60-day returns, or if the combined signal underperforms either proxy alone across rolling walk-forward tests.
